subject: Biology
title: Birdsong: Music with Meaning
description: A 60-second lesson on Birdsong: Music with Meaning.

hook.text: A nightingale can sing over 200 different song types, each with precise meaning.

buildup.text: Birds sing to defend territory, attract mates, and warn of danger.

discovery.text: Young birds learn songs from adults during a critical window. Isolated chicks sing garbled versions.

twist.text: City birds sing at higher pitches than rural ones to be heard over traffic noise.

climax.text: Birdsong shares brain pathways with human speech. Both require learning during development.

punchline.text: Birds compose, rehearse, and perform. Sound familiar?

quiz.question: Why do city birds sing at higher pitches?
options: To be heard over traffic noise
